description: Knowledge base of nonverbal/paralinguistic signals for the body-language reader. Each signal links an OBJECTIVELY MEASURABLE observable to PROBABILISTIC interpretations. Interpretations always carry confidence, required context, and caveats. This file is the product's interpretation layer and must never output a single deterministic verdict.

ekman_category.emblem: Gesture with a direct verbal translation; culture-specific (e.g., thumbs-up). Source: Ekman & Friesen 1969.
ekman_category.illustrator: Movement that accompanies/illustrates speech (e.g., beat gestures). Tied to speech, low standalone meaning.
ekman_category.affect_display: Face/body expression of emotion. Real but variable across people/culture/context (Barrett 2019).
ekman_category.regulator: Manages conversational flow (nods, gaze shifts, turn-taking cues).
ekman_category.adaptor: Self- or object-manipulation (self-touch, fidget). Popularly tied to anxiety/deception but EVIDENCE IS WEAK (DePaulo 2003).

evidence_strength.weak: Popular claim, small/mixed studies, NOT reliable for decisions.
evidence_strength.folklore: Trade-book/pop-psych assertion with no empirical backing. Hypothesis only.

legend.prior_confidence: Baseline P(state | signal) BEFORE fusing other modalities + context. Deliberately modest.

global_rules: Never emit a conclusion from a single signal. Require multi-signal constellations (Ekman 1969; cluster studies ~68% vs single-cue ~55%).
Distinguish communicative vs. non-communicative movement before interpreting (Wiener et al. 1972).
Always fuse scene/context; context alone adds +5-7% accuracy (EMOTIC 2020).
Prefer valence/arousal/dominance dimensions over hard emotion labels (AffectNet, EMOTIC).
Output soft-labels with confidence, not one verdict (AffectNet+ 2024).
Cultural/individual baseline required: calibrate against the person's own neutral baseline before flagging deviation.
Never produce deception/lie/intent verdicts; the science does not support it (DePaulo 2003). Surface observations, not judgments.

signals.label: Smile with eye-orbital activation (AU6+AU12, 'Duchenne')
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU6 (cheek raiser) + AU12 (lip corner puller) co-activation; MediaPipe face mesh for lip/cheek geometry
signals.observable: Lip corners raised AND cheeks raised with eye crinkle, sustained > ~0.5s

interpretations.caveats: Duchenne markers can be posed; presence != felt emotion (Barrett 2019).

signals.label: Inner+outer brow raise (AU1+AU2)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU1+AU2 intensity
signals.observable: Both brows raised, forehead horizontal lines

interpretations.caveats: Also used as conversational emphasis (illustrator) or greeting — not always emotion.
Highly ambiguous alone.

signals.label: Brow lowerer (AU4)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU4 intensity
signals.observable: Brows drawn down/together, vertical glabellar lines

interpretations.caveats: Frequently mistaken for anger.
AU4 alone is non-diagnostic.

signals.label: Lip-corner depressor (AU15)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU15 / MediaPipe blendshapes mouthFrownLeft+mouthFrownRight
signals.observable: Lip corners pulled downward vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Brief depressor can be momentary; corroborate with brow and voice.

signals.label: Upper-lid raiser / eye widen (AU5)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU5 / blendshapes eyeWideLeft+eyeWideRight
signals.observable: Visible sclera increased above upper iris; lids raised

interpretations.caveats: Also surprise, bright light, or simple attention. Ambiguous alone.

signals.label: Nose wrinkler / upper-lip raiser (AU9/AU10)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU9/AU10 / blendshapes noseSneer + mouthUpperUp
signals.observable: Nose wrinkled, upper lip raised, nasolabial deepening

interpretations.caveats: Disgust is among the more reliably recognized expressions, but AU9 can also signal general dislike/contempt.

signals.label: Lip press / lip stretch (AU23/AU24/AU20)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU23/24 / blendshapes mouthPress + mouthStretch
signals.observable: Lips pressed/tightened or stretched horizontally vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Also physical effort or concentration. NOT a deception cue.

signals.label: Jaw drop (AU26)
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace AU26 / blendshape jawOpen
signals.observable: Jaw lowered, mouth opens, not speaking

interpretations.caveats: Discount while the person is talking/yawning.

signals.label: Lip pucker / funneler (AU18/AU22)
signals.measurable_via: blendshapes mouthPucker + mouthFunnel
signals.observable: Lips pursed/pushed forward vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Also a speech shape or thinking gesture.

signals.label: Lip suck / bite (AU28)
signals.measurable_via: blendshapes mouthRollLower + mouthRollUpper
signals.observable: Lips drawn inward / bitten

interpretations.caveats: Habitual for many; not a deception cue.

signals.label: Chin raiser (AU17)
signals.measurable_via: blendshapes mouthShrugUpper + mouthShrugLower
signals.observable: Chin boss raised, lower lip pushed up (pout)

interpretations.caveats: Component of sadness; weak alone.

signals.label: Asymmetric smile / dimpler (AU12+AU14 unilateral)
signals.measurable_via: blendshape asymmetry |mouthSmileLeft - mouthSmileRight| + mouthDimple
signals.observable: One-sided lip-corner pull / dimple

interpretations.caveats: Unilateral lip action is the classic contempt marker, but also irony/playfulness.

signals.label: Jaw thrust (AU29)
signals.measurable_via: blendshape jawForward
signals.observable: Jaw pushed forward vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Also bite alignment / habit.

signals.label: Cheek puff (AU33/34)
signals.measurable_via: blendshape cheekPuff
signals.observable: Cheeks inflated, breath held/released

interpretations.caveats: Also physical exertion / blowing.

signals.label: Elevated blink rate (AU45 frequency)
signals.measurable_via: blendshape eyeBlink edge-count over a 6s window
signals.observable: Blink frequency above personal baseline (~0.3-0.4 Hz resting)

interpretations.caveats: Dry eyes, screens, contacts raise blink rate too. Very LOW rate suggests concentration.

signals.label: Happiness prototype (AU6+AU12)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: cheek raiser (AU6) + lip-corner puller (AU12)
signals.observable: Smile with genuine eye-orbital involvement

interpretations.caveats: Constellation beats single AUs, but smiles can still be posed/social (Barrett 2019).

signals.label: Sadness prototype (AU1+AU4+AU15+AU17)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: inner brow raise + brow lower + lip-corner depress + chin raise
signals.observable: Oblique brows, downturned mouth, raised chin

interpretations.caveats: Often brief; corroborate with voice/posture.

signals.label: Surprise prototype (AU1+AU2+AU5+AU26)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: full brow raise + upper-lid raise + jaw drop
signals.observable: Raised brows, wide eyes, dropped jaw

interpretations.caveats: Very brief; can blend into fear.

signals.label: Fear prototype (AU1+AU2+AU5+AU20)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: brow raise + upper-lid raise + lip stretch
signals.observable: Raised/drawn brows, widened eyes, stretched lips

interpretations.caveats: Overlaps surprise; hardest basic emotion to separate reliably.

signals.label: Anger prototype (AU4+AU5+AU7+AU23)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: brow lower + lid raise/tighten + lip press
signals.observable: Lowered drawn brows, hard stare, tightened lips

interpretations.caveats: Also intense concentration; context required.

signals.label: Disgust prototype (AU9+AU10+AU16)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: nose wrinkle + upper-lip raise + lower-lip depress
signals.observable: Wrinkled nose, raised upper lip

interpretations.caveats: Among the more recognizable; overlaps contempt.

signals.label: Contempt prototype (AU12+AU14 unilateral)
signals.measurable_via: constellation: one-sided lip-corner pull + dimpler
signals.observable: Unilateral lip tightening/raise

interpretations.caveats: Defined by one-sidedness; cross-cultural validity is debated.

signals.label: Gaze aversion / reduced eye contact
signals.measurable_via: OpenFace gaze vector + head pose; MediaPipe iris landmarks
signals.observable: Eye direction away from interlocutor beyond personal baseline

interpretations.caveats: Looking away aids memory retrieval — normal, not evasive.
Eye-contact norms are STRONGLY cultural; not a deception cue (DePaulo 2003).

signals.label: Head nod
signals.measurable_via: Head pose pitch oscillation (OpenFace/MediaPipe)
signals.observable: Repeated vertical head motion during listening/speaking

interpretations.caveats: Can be polite continuer, not true agreement; culture-varying (e.g., reversed in some regions).

signals.label: Forward torso lean
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: shoulder-hip vector angle vs vertical over time
signals.observable: Upper body inclined toward interlocutor relative to baseline

interpretations.caveats: Also occurs for hearing difficulty or aggression — context decides.

signals.label: Arms crossed over torso
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: wrist/elbow positions crossing torso midline
signals.observable: Forearms folded across chest

interpretations.caveats: POPULAR MYTH risk. Often just cold, comfortable, or habitual posture. Low diagnostic value.
Do not over-read.

signals.label: Hand-to-face self-touch (adaptor)
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe hand landmarks entering face-mesh region; frequency over time
signals.observable: Hand contacts face/mouth/neck, possibly repeated

interpretations.caveats: Classic 'tell' folklore. Adaptors rise with general arousal, NOT specifically with lying (DePaulo 2003). Report rate change, not a judgment.

signals.label: Backward lean
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: shoulder-vs-hip depth (z) inverted from forward lean
signals.observable: Upper body reclined away from interlocutor vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Also relaxation/comfort. Context decides.

signals.label: Shoulder shrug
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: shoulder-to-ear distance (neck length) shrinking vs baseline
signals.observable: Both shoulders raised toward ears, often brief

interpretations.caveats: Recognizable emblem but can be casual habit; localize culturally.

signals.label: Hands on hips (akimbo)
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: wrist near hip + elbow flared beyond shoulder line
signals.observable: One or both hands on hips, elbows out

interpretations.caveats: Also impatience or simple rest posture.

signals.label: Expansive / open posture
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: limb spread from torso center normalized by shoulder width
signals.observable: Arms/limbs spread wide, body takes up more space

interpretations.caveats: 'Power pose' effects are contested and poorly replicated. Treat cautiously.

signals.label: Lateral head tilt
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose/face: ear-line angle off horizontal
signals.observable: Head tilted to one side during interaction

interpretations.caveats: Also curiosity/evaluation; mild signal.

signals.label: Hand to neck / nape (adaptor)
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: wrist near neck/upper-chest midpoint
signals.observable: Hand touches neck, nape, or collar

interpretations.caveats: Comfort gesture; rises with general arousal, not specifically deception (DePaulo 2003).

signals.label: Fidget / restless hand motion
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe pose: wrist motion energy over a short window
signals.observable: Frequent small hand/arm movements vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Boredom, energy, or habit too. NOT a lie cue (DePaulo 2003).

signals.label: Open palm(s) shown
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe hand landmarks: fingers extended/spread, palm toward viewer
signals.observable: Open hand(s), fingers apart, palm visible

interpretations.caveats: Cultural/illustrative; weak standalone.

signals.label: Hands together / steepled / clasped
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe hand landmarks: two-hand centroids close
signals.observable: Hands brought together (steeple, prayer, or clasp)

interpretations.caveats: Steepling≈confidence vs tight clasp≈tension — needs finer detail to disambiguate.

signals.label: Pointing / index extension
signals.measurable_via: MediaPipe hand landmarks: index extended, other fingers folded
signals.observable: Index finger extended toward a target/person

interpretations.caveats: Illustrator/deictic; can read as aggressive in some cultures.

signals.label: Emblem (e.g., thumbs-up, OK-sign, wave)
signals.measurable_via: Hand-gesture classifier (e.g., trained on Jester/IPN Hand) over MediaPipe hand landmarks
signals.observable: Discrete recognizable hand configuration with conventional meaning

interpretations.caveats: Meaning is culturally encoded and can invert across cultures. Highest-confidence signal type BUT must localize.

signals.label: Beat / illustrator gestures with speech
signals.measurable_via: Hand motion energy time-aligned with speech onsets (MediaPipe + VAD)
signals.observable: Rhythmic hand movements synchronized to speech prosody

interpretations.caveats: Rate varies by individual/culture; reduced illustrators can mean disengagement OR careful speech.

signals.label: Rising pitch / increased F0 variance
signals.measurable_via: openSMILE eGeMAPS: F0 mean & variance, jitter
signals.observable: Elevated/variable fundamental frequency vs personal baseline

interpretations.caveats: Arousal != valence. Rising pitch can't distinguish excited from anxious alone.

signals.label: Filled pauses / speech disfluencies
signals.measurable_via: Whisper transcript timing + filler detection; openSMILE pause stats
signals.observable: Increased 'um/uh', longer silences, restarts vs baseline

interpretations.caveats: Indicates effort/uncertainty, NOT deception. Many honest speakers are disfluent.

signals.label: Scene / situational context modifier
signals.measurable_via: Scene classifier + object/activity detection (EMOTIC-style)
signals.observable: Setting (interview, casual, crowd), activity, social configuration

interpretations.caveats: Context is not a state itself; it reweights other signals' priors. e.g., crossed arms outdoors in winter => downweight 'defensive'. EMOTIC shows context adds +5-7% mAP.

constructs._doc: EVIDENCE-BACKED HIGHER-LEVEL READS. Each construct is a constellation: multiple independent signals vote for it, and confidence scales with how many corroborate. This is the scientifically supported way to read behavior — single cues are weak (~55%, DePaulo 2003) but cue constellations are far stronger (thin-slice r≈.39, Ambady & Rosenthal 1992; multimodal engagement/stress detection 0.7–0.93). A construct with >=min_cues active signals is treated as corroborated (confident), not hedged.

engagement.label: Engagement / Interest
engagement.evidence: Thin-slice behavior predicts engagement/interpersonal outcomes at r≈.39 (Ambady & Rosenthal 1992); multimodal classroom engagement detection ≈90%+ fusing face+posture+gesture.

discomfort_anxiety.label: Discomfort / Anxiety
discomfort_anxiety.evidence: Self-directed displacement behaviours (self-touch, fidget) are reliably read as stress (signal value of stress behaviour, 2022); hands/eyes/mouth/torso are the most salient anxiety cues, r≈.50.
sources: Nonverbal cues for anxiety / emotional leakage

confidence_dominance.label: Confidence / Dominance
confidence_dominance.evidence: The reliably-recognized dominance display is a multi-cue constellation: bodily expansiveness (expanded posture, arms out, hands on hips) + downward head tilt + no smile — perceived from coordinated multi-channel cues, NOT single signals (Witkower & Tracy 2019).

disengagement.label: Disengagement / Withdrawal
disengagement.evidence: Contractive/closed posture + gaze aversion signal low involvement; the inverse of the engagement constellation (Mehrabian immediacy; Ambady & Rosenthal 1992).

rapport_openness.label: Rapport / Openness
rapport_openness.evidence: Rapport is judged from expressivity, attentiveness, positivity, and coordination; low rapport detectable from facial cues at avg precision 0.7 (Grahe & Bernieri 1999; Müller et al. 2018).

fusion_policy.method: Combine per-signal priors across modalities (face, body, gesture, prosody, content) weighted by inference_reliability, then reweight by context modifiers, then calibrate to the subject's personal baseline.
fusion_policy.output_format: Ranked soft-labels over valence/arousal/dominance + discrete states, each with a confidence and the contributing signals listed for transparency.
hard_constraints: No output above 'observation' tier without >=2 corroborating signals.
Always show confidence and the evidence_strength of contributing signals.
